questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next letter in the series: A, C, E, G, ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The letters advance by two positions each time (odd-positioned letters).

**Working.** After G (7th letter), skip H to reach I.

**Answer.** I.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: Finally, the processed ore is transported to the smelter.
Q: First, the ore body is located through exploratory drilling.
R: Then the ore is extracted and hauled to the surface.
S: Next, it is crushed and concentrated at the mill.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order the steps by their explicit sequence markers (First, Then, Next, Finally).

**Working.** First locate (Q), then extract and haul (R), next crush/concentrate (S), finally transport to smelter (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: No metals are gases. All mercury is metal. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine a universal negative with a universal affirmative.

**Working.** Mercury ⊆ metals and metals ∩ gases $=\varnothing$, so no mercury is a gas.

**Answer.** No mercury is gas.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Given 'All P are Q' and 'All Q are R' (with P non-empty), which of the following necessarily follow?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply transitivity and existential import of a non-empty set.

**Working.** P ⊆ Q ⊆ R gives 'All P are R'; since P is non-empty, 'Some R are P' holds; 'All Q are R' gives 'Some Q are R'. 'All R are P' is not implied (R may be larger).

**Answer.** All P are R; Some R are P; Some Q are R.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the option that best captures the meaning of 'EQUIVOCAL' in: 'The survey gave equivocal results.'
options: open to more than one interpretation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Equivocal' means ambiguous or capable of being understood in more than one way.

**Working.** Equivocal results are uncertain and admit multiple interpretations; the antonym would be 'unequivocal' (clear).

**Answer.** open to more than one interpretation.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $5, 11, 23, 47, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term follows $a_{n+1}=2a_n+1$.

**Working.** $47\times 2 + 1 = 95$.

**Answer.** $95$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a row of $20$ students, A is $7$th from the left. A's position from the right is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Position from right $=$ total $-$ position from left $+1$.

**Working.** $20-7+1=14$.

**Answer.** 14th.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: At sunrise, the shadow of a vertical pole falls toward which direction?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A shadow points away from the sun.

**Working.** At sunrise the sun is in the east, so the shadow falls toward the west.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The relationship among Doctors, Women and Engineers (each pair can share members) is best shown by
options: two overlapping circles and one separate
questions.solution:
**Concept.** When all three categories can share members, all pairs overlap.

**Working.** A person can be a woman, a doctor and/or an engineer, so all three circles intersect.

**Answer.** three mutually overlapping circles.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with exactly three painted faces is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with its eight corners highlighted.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Three-face cubes are the corner cubes.

**Working.** A cube has $8$ corners, so $8$ small cubes have three painted faces.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The triaxial stress factor $N=\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$ for $\phi=30^\circ$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $N=\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$.

**Working.**
$$N=\tan^2 60^\circ=(\sqrt{3})^2=3.$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A total airflow of $30\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ splits naturally between two parallel airways of resistance $R_1=0.1$ and $R_2=0.4\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$. Compute the quantity through the higher-resistance airway ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Natural splitting of airflow
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='10' y1='70' x2='60' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#sa)'/><path d='M60 70 L60 35 L190 35' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M60 70 L60 105 L190 105' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='190' y1='35' x2='230' y2='35' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#sa)'/><line x1='190' y1='105' x2='230' y2='105' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#sa)'/><defs><marker id='sa' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='100' y='30' font-size='8'>R1=0.1</text><text x='100' y='125' font-size='8'>R2=0.4, total Q=30</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a free split, equal pressure drop gives $\dfrac{Q_1}{Q_2}=\sqrt{\dfrac{R_2}{R_1}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{Q_1}{Q_2}=\sqrt{\frac{0.4}{0.1}}=2\Rightarrow Q_1=2Q_2,\ Q_1+Q_2=30\Rightarrow Q_2=10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A line has the whole-circle bearing of $210^\circ$ shown on the compass. Its reduced (quadrantal) bearing is

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='80' cy='80' r='60' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='80' y1='15' x2='80' y2='145' stroke='#cbd5e1'/><line x1='15' y1='80' x2='145' y2='80' stroke='#cbd5e1'/><text x='74' y='12' font-size='10'>N</text><text x='74' y='156' font-size='10'>S</text><line x1='80' y1='80' x2='50' y2='132' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bn2)'/><defs><marker id='bn2' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='88' y='104' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>WCB 210°</text></svg>
figure.caption: Whole-circle bearing of a line

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For WCB between $180^\circ$ and $270^\circ$ (third quadrant) the reduced bearing is $S\,(\text{WCB}-180^\circ)\,W$.

**Reasoning.** $210^\circ-180^\circ=30^\circ$, so the line lies in the SW quadrant at $30^\circ$ from south.

**Answer.** $S\,30^\circ\,W$.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: For the same slope distance of $100$ m at an inclination of $30^\circ$, compute the vertical component (difference in level) in metres.

figure.caption: Vertical component of slope line
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='200' y2='40' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='200' y1='40' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='40' y='114' font-size='8'>30 deg</text><text x='205' y='85' font-size='8'>V</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Vertical component $V=L\sin\theta$.

**Working.**
$$V=100\sin30^\circ=100\times0.5=50\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $50$ m.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A row of the pattern shown is 40 m long with holes at 4 m spacing. How many holes are in the row?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holes in a row = length ÷ spacing + 1.

**Working.**
$$N = \frac{40}{4} + 1 = 10 + 1 = 11$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{11}$ holes.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: A project requires an initial outlay of Rs $800000$ and returns a uniform Rs $200000$ per year (figure). Compute the simple payback period (years).

figure.caption: Cumulative inflow
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='200' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='22' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='22' height='40'/><rect x='115' y='50' width='22' height='60'/><rect x='150' y='30' width='22' height='80'/></g><text x='42' y='128' font-size='8'>cumulative inflow 200000/yr</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Payback $=\dfrac{\text{initial outlay}}{\text{annual inflow}}$.

**Working.**
$$t=\frac{800000}{200000}=4\ \text{years}$$

**Answer.** $4$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan characteristic $p=300-10Q$ (Pa) operates against a mine of resistance giving $p=2Q^2$ (Pa), as shown. The operating-point quantity Q, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.

figure.caption: Fan characteristic and mine resistance curve

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the operating point the fan pressure equals the mine resistance pressure: $a-bQ=rQ^2$.

**Working.**
$$2Q^2+10Q-300=0\Rightarrow Q^2+5Q-150=0$$
$$Q=\frac{-5+\sqrt{25+600}}{2}=\frac{-5+25}{2}=10$$

**Answer.** $10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A blasthole must break 240 m³ of rock at a powder factor of 0.5 kg/m³. The required charge per hole, in kg, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=PF\times V$.

**Working.**
$$Q=0.5\times240=120\ \text{kg}$$

**Answer.** 120 kg.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: From a station the bearings of two lines are 60° and 150°. The included angle between the lines, in degrees, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Included angle $=$ difference of the two bearings.

**Working.**
$$150-60=90^{\circ}$$

**Answer.** 90°.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: If Graham's ratio at a sealed area rises from 0.5 to 2.0, the factor by which it has increased is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Factor $=\dfrac{\text{new value}}{\text{old value}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{2.0}{0.5}=4$$

**Answer.** 4 (a strong rise indicating intensifying heating).

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A cemented backfill of 1000 t is mixed to a 5% cement content by mass. The cement required, in t, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cement mass $=$ fill mass $\times$ cement fraction.

**Working.**
$$m=1000\times0.05=50\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** 50 t.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The system $x+y=2,\; x+ky=4$ fails to have a unique solution when $k=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $2\times2$ system has a unique solution iff the coefficient determinant is non-zero.

**Working.**
$$\det\begin{bmatrix}1 & 1\\ 1 & k\end{bmatrix}=k-1=0\;\Rightarrow\;k=1$$
At $k=1$ the lines are parallel (here inconsistent), so no unique solution.

**Answer.** $k=1$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: In good blast-pattern design the spacing-to-burden ratio (S/B) is usually kept around
questions.solution:
**Concept.** S/B controls fragmentation uniformity.

**Reasoning.** Field practice and design rules (e.g. Konya) keep S/B between about 1.1 and 1.5; too low wastes holes, too high leaves coarse rock between holes.

**Answer.** About 1.1–1.5.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: The prismoidal correction applied to an earthwork volume
options: increases the end-area volume to a larger value always
adjusts the (usually larger) end-area volume to the more accurate prismoidal volume
is the same as the closing error
questions.solution:
**Concept.** End-area overestimates for non-linear sections.

**Reasoning.** The trapezoidal (end-area) method generally gives a slightly larger volume than the true prismoid; the prismoidal correction is subtracted to obtain the accurate volume.

**Answer.** Refines end-area to prismoidal volume.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A roof bolt must suspend the loose rock block shown (1.5 m × 1.5 m plan, 1 m thick, unit weight 25 kN/m³). Compute the dead load (kN) carried by one bolt supporting this block.

figure.caption: Suspension bolting
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='160' height='30' fill='#a16207'/><rect x='30' y='60' width='160' height='40' fill='#d6c0a3' stroke='#92400e'/><text x='70' y='85' font-size='8'>loose rock block</text><line x1='110' y1='100' x2='110' y2='45' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='3'/><text x='34' y='130' font-size='8'>block 1.5x1.5x1 m, 25 kN/m^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dead load = unit weight × block volume.

**Working.**
$$W = 25 \times (1.5 \times 1.5 \times 1) = 25 \times 2.25 = 56.25$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{56.25}$ kN.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For downcast density $1.18\,\text{kg/m}^3$, upcast $1.10\,\text{kg/m}^3$ and depth $150\,\text{m}$, the natural ventilation pressure ($g=9.81$) is _____ Pa. Round to one decimal.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{NVP}=(\rho_d-\rho_u)gH$.

**Working.**
$$(1.18-1.10)\times9.81\times150=0.08\times9.81\times150=117.7.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 117.7\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Pillar stress is $50\,\text{MPa}$ when the vertical field stress is $10\,\text{MPa}$. Using $\sigma_p=\sigma_v/(1-e)$, the extraction ratio $e$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $1-e=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{\sigma_p}$.

**Working.**
$$1-e=\frac{10}{50}=0.2,\quad e=0.8.$$

**Answer.** $0.8$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The Brazilian test on a rock disc is used to determine the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Diametral compression induces tensile splitting.

**Working.** The failure load gives the indirect (splitting) tensile strength.

**Answer.** indirect tensile strength.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: In the section shown, tilted older strata are truncated and overlain by flat-lying younger beds (figure). This surface is:

figure.caption: Angular unconformity
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='#475569'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='200' y2='60'/><line x1='20' y1='72' x2='200' y2='72'/></g><path d='M20 90 Q110 70 200 90' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><g stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='90' y2='130'/><line x1='110' y1='100' x2='170' y2='125'/></g><text x='28' y='148' font-size='8'>tilted beds below, flat above</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unconformities mark gaps in the record.

**Reasoning.** Tilted, eroded older beds overlain by flat younger beds at an angular discordance define an angular unconformity.

**Answer.** An angular unconformity.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Basalt, which forms the Deccan Traps of India, is a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Basalt is the commonest extrusive rock.

**Reasoning.** It is fine-grained, dark, mafic (rich in Fe, Mg, plagioclase, pyroxene) and erupts as fluid lava; vast flows built the Deccan Traps.

**Answer.** Fine-grained basic volcanic rock.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A key advantage of the Nonel (shock-tube) initiation system over electric detonators is that it is
options: immune to stray electric currents, static and radio-frequency hazards
able to work without any detonator
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shock tube carries a low-energy signal, not electricity.

**Reasoning.** A thin reactive dust layer inside the plastic tube propagates a shock wave to the detonator; since no electric current is involved, it is unaffected by stray currents, static or RF — a major safety benefit.

**Answer.** Immune to stray/electric hazards.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: If the loose ore density is 1.8 t/m³, the LHD output of 144 m³/h corresponds to ____ t/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ volume rate $\times$ loose density.

**Working.**
$$Q=144\times1.8=259.2\ \text{t/h}$$

**Answer.** 259.2 t/h.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Using a rule of thumb of burden = 30 × hole diameter, the burden for a 150 mm hole, in m, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $B=30\,d$ (a common opencast guideline).

**Working.**
$$B=30\times0.15=4.5\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 4.5 m.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Which of the following are modern geomatics techniques used to obtain position or spatial data? (Select all that apply.)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Geomatics covers instruments and platforms that acquire spatial data.

**Reasoning.** Total stations, GNSS and photogrammetry/remote sensing all yield positional or spatial information. Bowditch's rule is a traverse-adjustment computation, not a data-acquisition technique.

**Answer.** Total station, GNSS, photogrammetry/remote sensing.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: For the same fault (net slip $20\ \text{m}$, fault dip $30^\circ$), compute the horizontal heave (m).

figure.caption: Horizontal heave of a fault
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='40' y1='30' x2='170' y2='140' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5 3'/><g stroke='#b45309' stroke-width='3'><line x1='20' y1='70' x2='95' y2='95'/><line x1='120' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120'/></g><line x1='95' y1='110' x2='150' y2='110' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='28' y='152' font-size='8'>heave = horizontal offset</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Heave $=$ net slip $\times\cos(\text{fault dip})$.

**Working.**
$$\text{heave}=20\cos30^\circ=20\times0.8660=17.32\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $\approx 17.3\ \text{m}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of equal resistance $R$ in parallel have an equivalent resistance of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\dfrac{2}{\sqrt R}\Rightarrow R_{eq}=R/4$.

**Working.** Two equal parallel airways quarter the resistance.

**Answer.** $R/4$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A $200\,\text{cm}$ core run contains intact pieces $\ge 10\,\text{cm}$ summing to $150\,\text{cm}$. The RQD is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{RQD}=\dfrac{\sum(\ge10\,\text{cm})}{\text{total}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\text{RQD}=\frac{150}{200}\times100=75\%.$$

**Answer.** $75\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air flows round the right-angle bend shown, where the shock-loss factor is $X=1.5$ and the velocity pressure is $p_v=40\ \mathrm{Pa}$. The shock (bend) pressure loss, in Pa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M30 40 L120 40 L120 150' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='16'/><path d='M30 40 L120 40 L120 150' fill='none' stroke='#e0f2fe' stroke-width='9'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bf)'/><line x1='120' y1='75' x2='120' y2='135' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bf)'/><defs><marker id='bf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='40' y='95' font-size='9'>X = 1.5</text><text x='40' y='110' font-size='9'>pv = 40 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Right-angle bend in an airway

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shock loss $=X\,p_v$.

**Working.**
$$\Delta p=1.5\times40=60\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $60$ Pa.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The kinetic energy of a $2\,\text{kg}$ body moving at $10\,\text{m/s}$ is _____ J.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $KE=\tfrac12 mv^2$.

**Working.**
$$KE=\tfrac12(2)(10)^2=100.$$

**Answer.** $100\,\text{J}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $600\,\text{Pa}$ and the natural ventilation pressure of $100\,\text{Pa}$ acts in the same direction. The total motive pressure is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Aiding NVP adds to fan pressure.

**Working.**
$$600+100=700.$$

**Answer.** $700\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The fan characteristic is $P=150-10Q$ and the mine resistance line is $P=4Q^2$ (see figure). The operating airflow $Q$, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The operating point is where fan pressure equals system resistance.

**Working.**
$$150-10Q=4Q^2\Rightarrow 4Q^2+10Q-150=0\Rightarrow 2Q^2+5Q-75=0$$
$$Q=\frac{-5+\sqrt{25+600}}{4}=\frac{-5+25}{4}=5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The eigenvalues of an invertible matrix $A$ are $2$ and $5$. The smallest eigenvalue of $A^{-1}$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** If $\lambda$ is an eigenvalue of $A$, then $1/\lambda$ is an eigenvalue of $A^{-1}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{eigenvalues of }A^{-1}=\tfrac12,\;\tfrac15$$
The smallest is $\tfrac15=0.2$.

**Answer.** $0.2$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Compared with a drum winder of equivalent rope, a friction (Koepe) winder is limited in maximum payload mainly because
options: drive depends on friction grip, so excessive load would cause the rope to slip on the sheave
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Traction drive has a slip limit.

**Reasoning.** The Koepe winder transmits force only through friction between rope and sheave; if the load (or out-of-balance) exceeds the available traction, the rope slips. Payload is therefore capped by the friction grip, unlike a positively wound drum.

**Answer.** Friction grip limits load to avoid rope slip.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Bauxite and laterite are typical
options: residual deposits formed by intense chemical weathering that removes soluble matter and leaves an insoluble enriched residue
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Weathering can concentrate ore in place.

**Reasoning.** In warm, humid climates leaching removes silica and alkalis, leaving Al/Fe-rich residues; bauxite (Al ore) and laterite are the classic residual deposits.

**Answer.** Residual (weathering) deposits.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: An 'angular unconformity' is one in which
options: the beds above and below are parallel
tilted/folded older strata are overlain, across an erosion surface, by younger beds at a different angle
younger sediments rest on an igneous/metamorphic basement
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angular unconformity shows discordant attitudes.

**Reasoning.** The lower beds were tilted/folded and eroded before the upper, gently dipping beds were deposited, so they meet at an angle; a disconformity has parallel beds, a nonconformity has sediments on crystalline basement.

**Answer.** Discordant (angular) contact.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project earns an average annual profit of $\textsf{Rs}\,18{,}000$ on an average investment of $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$. The accounting rate of return is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ARR $=\dfrac{\text{avg profit}}{\text{avg investment}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{18000}{50000}=0.36=36\%.$$

**Answer.** $36\%$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the $100\,\text{N}$ block on a $20^\circ$ incline, the normal reaction is _____ N. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Normal reaction $=W\cos\theta$.

**Working.**
$$N=100\cos 20^\circ=93.97.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 93.97\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Surface mining of an increment remains economic as long as its
options: actual stripping ratio is less than or equal to the break-even stripping ratio
actual stripping ratio exceeds the break-even stripping ratio
bench height equals the berm width
overall slope angle exceeds the face angle
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The pit limit is set where actual SR equals BESR.

**Reasoning.** While the waste-to-ore ratio of the next increment is at or below the break-even value, the increment pays its way; once it exceeds BESR, further surface mining loses money and defines the ultimate pit limit.

**Answer.** Actual SR $\le$ BESR.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways, each of resistance $2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$, are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For two equal resistances in parallel, $R_{eq}=R/4$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{2}{4}=0.5.$$

**Answer.** $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The shear strength available on a plane is $50\,\text{kPa}$ and the mobilised (driving) shear stress is $25\,\text{kPa}$. The factor of safety is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\tau_{available}}{\tau_{driving}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{50}{25}=2.$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: When directly fighting an underground fire, a key precaution is to
options: reverse the main ventilation immediately in every case
maintain controlled ventilation, approach from the intake (fresh-air) side and prevent accumulation of explosive gas behind the firefighters
seal the area while persons are still inside
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Firefighter safety depends on fresh air and gas control.

**Reasoning.** Attacking from the intake keeps firefighters in fresh air with the smoke carried away; ventilation must be controlled to avoid building explosive mixtures or spreading the fire, with continuous gas monitoring.

**Answer.** Keep ventilation, attack from the intake side.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: 1000 m³ of rock (bank volume) swells by 25% on excavation. The loose (bulked) volume, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose volume $=$ bank volume $\times(1+\text{swell})$.

**Working.**
$$V_L=1000\,(1+0.25)=1250\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 1250 m³.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Using Taylor's rule, life $\approx 6.5\,(\text{tonnage in Mt})^{0.25}$ years. For a reserve of $16$ million tonnes, the estimated mine life is _____ years.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: An overall pit slope rises $H=120\ \text{m}$ over a horizontal projection of $80\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the overall slope angle (degrees, two decimals).

figure.caption: Overall pit slope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,30 30,120 190,120' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='190' y2='120' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='70' font-size='8'>H=120</text><text x='95' y='133' font-size='8'>horizontal 80</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\theta=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{H}{\text{horizontal}}\right)$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{120}{80}\right)=\tan^{-1}(1.5)=56.31^\circ$$

**Answer.** $56.31^\circ$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: With overburden depth $H=200\,\text{m}$ and angle of draw $25^\circ$, the horizontal distance the subsidence trough extends beyond the panel edge ($H\tan 25^\circ$) is _____ m. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Limit offset $=H\tan(\text{angle of draw})$.

**Working.**
$$200\tan 25^\circ=200(0.4663)=93.26.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 93.26\,\text{m}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f=x^{2}+yz$, the directional derivative at $(1,1,1)$ in the direction of $\mathbf{a}=3\hat{j}+4\hat{k}$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Using $PPV=K\,(SD)^{-n}$ with $K=1140$, $n=1.6$ and $SD=14.14$ m/kg$^{0.5}$, the predicted PPV, in mm/s, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Empirical attenuation law $PPV=K\,SD^{-n}$.

**Working.**
$$SD^{1.6}=14.14^{1.6}=69.3$$
$$PPV=\frac{1140}{69.3}=16.45\ \text{mm/s}$$

**Answer.** 16.45 mm/s.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A sampler's filter gains 0.6 mg of dust after sampling 300 litres (0.3 m³) of air. The respirable dust concentration, in mg/m³, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Concentration $=\dfrac{\text{mass collected}}{\text{air volume}}$.

**Working.**
$$c=\frac{0.6}{0.3}=2.0\ \text{mg/m}^3$$

**Answer.** 2.0 mg/m³.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A shovel's theoretical bank output is 400 m³/h. With availability 0.8 and utilization 0.75, its effective bank output, in m³/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Effective output $=$ theoretical $\times$ availability $\times$ utilization.

**Working.**
$$Q_{eff}=400\times0.8\times0.75=240\ \text{m}^3/\text{h}$$

**Answer.** 240 m³/h.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Large opencast benches generally use large-diameter blastholes (150–310 mm) because
options: they are cheaper to charge by hand
larger holes give higher drilling productivity and allow larger burdens/spacings and fewer holes per volume
they reduce the powder factor to zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Hole diameter scales the blast pattern.

**Reasoning.** A larger hole holds more explosive per metre, permitting larger burden and spacing, so fewer holes are needed to blast a given volume and drilling productivity per m³ rises — economic on big benches.

**Answer.** Higher productivity and larger patterns.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: A sum of $\textsf{Rs}\,100{,}000$ is to be received $2$ years from now. At $10\%$ per annum, compute its present worth ($\textsf{Rs}$).

figure.caption: Present worth of a future sum
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='220' y2='80' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='180' y1='80' x2='180' y2='35' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ar)'/><defs><marker id='ar' markerWidth='7' markerHeight='7' refX='4' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L5 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='158' y='28' font-size='8'>F at year 2</text><text x='20' y='110' font-size='8'>find P now, i=10%</text></svg>

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Flattening (reducing) the overall pit slope angle generally
options: reduces waste stripping but lowers slope stability
increases waste stripping but improves slope stability
has no effect on either waste or stability
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Slope angle trades stripping cost against stability.

**Reasoning.** A flatter wall pushes the crest back, exposing more waste to be removed (higher SR), but the lower, less-steep wall has a higher factor of safety.

**Answer.** More waste but greater stability.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In sublevel caving, the ore is
options: mined in open stopes with no caving
blasted by ring drilling on sublevels and drawn beneath a cap of caved waste, so dilution from waste is inherent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sublevel caving caves the waste, not the ore.

**Reasoning.** Rings of upward holes are drilled and blasted on sublevels; the ore is drawn under an overlying mat of caved waste that follows it down — so some waste dilution is unavoidable and draw control is essential.

**Answer.** Ring-blasted ore drawn under caved waste (inherent dilution).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The internal rate of return of a project is the discount rate at which
options: the net present value equals zero
the payback period is one year
questions.solution:
**Concept.** IRR is the breakeven discount rate.

**Working.** At IRR, $\text{NPV}=0$.

**Answer.** the net present value equals zero.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Gaussian elimination transforms a coefficient matrix into
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Forward elimination produces zeros below the pivots.

**Working.** The result is an upper-triangular system solved by back-substitution.

**Answer.** An upper-triangular matrix.
